history.qwen_rationale: Forcing elasticsearch-rest-client to 8.12.1 aligns the dependency with the code's use of Apache HttpClient 5.x and matches Spring Boot 3.3's managed version, resolving the RestClient.builder signature mismatch before the framework upgrade runs.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed in ElasticsearchConfig.java because RestClient.builder() expects org.apache.hc.core5.http.HttpHost[] (HttpClient 5) but the classpath contains an older elasticsearch-rest-client using HttpClient 4 (org.apache.http.HttpHost).
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.3 manages Elasticsearch clients at version 8.13.4, which uses Apache HttpClient 5. The project likely pins an older version or the SB upgrade didn't override it. Explicitly upgrading elasticsearch-rest-client (and elasticsearch-java) to 8.13.4 before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade ensures the correct HttpClient 5-compatible API is on the classpath during compilation.

history.qwen_rationale: The UpgradeDependencyVersion recipe likely failed to override a BOM-managed or direct dependency declaration.
